title: Hardcore IndieWeb: Run your own website 100% independently for only $0.01/day
content_html:
Adam Newbold
July 18, 2026

If you’re not familiar with the IndieWeb movement, it’s a practical approach to maintaining your presence on the web that emphasizes fully owning your identity and your content. This is especially relevant now, at a time when a growing number of corporations want to own and control those things for you. The IndieWeb ethos seeks to eliminate external influence and control over both you and your stuff.

And as with any movement, there are a bunch of different services out there aiming to help you plug into this modern independent web. I even run one myself! But I always place my values over profit, so I don’t have any problem sharing the simple truth that you don’t need a fancy subscription blogging service to join the IndieWeb. In fact, when you use one of those services, you’re actually taking a step back from real independence, because your content often winds up living in someone else’s database on someone else’s server. They’ll tell you that’s OK because you can always export it whenever you’d like, in open formats, and of course that’s nice. But you still don’t fully fully own and control your content when you’re dependent on someone else’s service.

There’s nothing wrong with using those services, to be clear. And if you’re already using them and happy with them, that’s great! This guide isn’t for you. But if the idea of having complete independence and control over your content on the web is appealing to you, read on. Because that’s what Hardcore IndieWeb is all about.

Defining Hardcore IndieWeb

Hardcore IndieWeb fully embraces existing IndieWeb principles; there’s really nothing new or different there. The key aspects of Hardcore IndieWeb are control over your content and portability of your website:

  • If your content doesn’t primarily exist on your own hard drive, you don’t fully control your content.
  • If you don’t have a copy of your published HTML and web assets on your own hard drive, it’s not truly portable.

Why does this matter? Consider what happens if the IndieWeb blogging service you’re using goes belly-up one day. Your ability to move to a new service depends entirely on your ability to export your data. What if you can’t export it? The service provider may have told you that you own your content, but what good does that do if you can’t access it?

From a portability standpoint, consider what happens if you discover that the owner of your IndieWeb blogging service has been outed as engaging in abhorrent behavior or just comes down with a major case of the ick (this has happened before!). Sure, you can export your data in an open format and move house. But now you have to find another service that can work with that format, or convert everything and adopt an entirely new set of tools or processes. And you have to move house, which is a major pain.

If you follow Hardcore IndieWeb, neither of these situations can affect you. Your content remains completely under your own control at all times, and your website exists in a fully published format at all times. The independence is tangible, and you can benefit from it whenever necessary.

The methodology

In many ways—no, actually, in every way—Hardcore IndieWeb mirrors the very same web publishing practices that were used in the 90s when the web was brand new. These were things that I did back then myself, every day, and they worked great! And they still work today, of course, but few people would recognize the approach because we’re now multiple levels deep in hybrid SaaS CMS/SSG solutions tethered to half a dozen markup languages and twice as many template systems. We’re living in a messy and complex web right now, but it doesn’t have to be that way. The complexity is one choice, and the simplicity of Hardcore IndieWeb is a different choice.

The process looks like this:

  1. Author your web content on your hard drive.
  2. Preview it in your web browser.
  3. When you’re happy with what you have, upload it to your web host. Repeat as often as you’d like.

That’s it. It really is that simple. Besides a domain name, here’s what you’ll need:

  • A text editor
  • A file transfer tool
  • A web host

Again, that’s all. You don’t need any other special software. No programming environments or IDEs, no frameworks or shells or CLI tools. And definitely no monthly subscriptions. In the next section, I’ll cover choosing these items, and I’ll suggest a very nice web host that literally only costs $0.01/day, which you can pay-as-you-go (so no subscription).

(You do also need to know some HTML, but everyone can learn HTML. And in reality, you can get away with only knowing a few tags and largely copying and pasting, so don’t sweat it!)

Getting Started

First, you’ll need to find a text editor that you like. Hopefully you already have one, but if not, you can try different editors until you find one that works for you. (I use Nova by Panic, myself.) You can use virtually any editor, because it only needs to support saving files to your disk (and they all do that).

Then, you’ll need a way to transfer files. Part of the reason I use Nova as my editor is that it handles file transfers, too. But there are plenty of options out there. FileZilla is a nice cross-platform app. You can use anything that can transfer files over SSH (or the SFTP protocol).

Finally, you’ll need a place to host your web content. There are a zillion web hosts out there, all with various features and benefits. But for Hardcore IndieWeb, you only need the basics, and you don’t need to spend a lot of money at all. For just $0.01/day, you can run a static website at NearlyFreeSpeech.net, and that’s what I recommend. I’ve had an active account with them since 2008 and they’re awesome!

You can use any web host you’d like, though. If you want to use NearlyFreeSpeech, go ahead and sign up, fund your account (you can kick things off with as little as $0.25), add a site (static, non-production), and you’re good to go. You’ll find your site’s login information (for file transfers) on the Sites tab after clicking on your site name. You’ll get a free subdomain to work with, but you can add your own personal domain on the Domains tab if you’d like (and you should totally do that).

The Process

Once you have your editor, a way to transfer files, and an active account with a web host, you’re ready to get going. At this point, the world is your oyster. You can run any kind of website you’d like, but for this guide, we’ll assume you want to run a blog.

Most of the time, a blog consists of these things:

  • A landing page
  • Posts/articles
  • An archive page
  • A feed

If you’ve always assumed that you need a special blogging service or software to maintain these items, that’s totally understandable. But you don’t! You can actually manage these yourself, and it’s really super easy. At this point you might (understandably) be wondering “what kind of person manages their own RSS feed in a text editor?” And the answer is you. You are the kind of person who is totally capable of managing your own RSS feed in your own editor.

We’ll cover that shortly. First, let’s cover the HTML pages.

A Quick Note About Web Pages

If you already have your existing web site or blog in HTML format, you’re in a great position to get started with Hardcore IndieWeb. If you don’t, that’s OK. Depending on where your blog lives today, there are likely ways to export it in HTML or to convert it into HTML. If your blog is huge, you’ll probably want to use a tool to make this easier. If it’s small, maybe you’d enjoy diving into creating your new HTML files yourself (which is always a fun way to revisit your posts and such).

If you don’t know much HTML, you can totally learn it. Heck, I’ll even teach you if you’d like. It’s super fun to learn, and using HTML brings you so much closer to your web content in ways that modern tools just don’t capture. Markdown may be many people’s preferred way to author content, but HTML is the language of the web, and I can personally attest to the fact that there are plenty of occasions where doing something in plain HTML turned out to be far easier than fighting a Markdown parser.

Anyway. If you want to jump in with some ready-made web material to use as a basis for your site, there are a bajillion free web designs and templates out there. Here’s one I just found. You can download something you like, open it in your text editor, and make it your very own.

Landing Page

Your landing page is your home on the web, and you can do whatever you’d like with it. It can show your latest blog post, or several of them, or none of them at all. It can show entire posts or just the first couple of sentences. You’re in complete control here, and you can put whatever you want on that page. If you want to display your latest blog post, just copy and paste it there and add a link to its standalone page. If you want to show your five most recent posts, paste the new one at the top and delete the oldest one at the bottom. You’re now working directly with your content on your own landing page, and you can do whatever you’d like, however you’d like.

This might sound like madness, if you’re fully accustomed to today’s modern template-driven systems for managing blogs and web content. And that’s a totally fair way to feel about it. But consider that this approach offers some unique possibilities that are actually harder to do with template-driven solutions. With this approach, you’re in a position to deviate from the norm. You can take things in different directions. You can apply your creativity in fun and interesting ways, and you’ll never find yourself fighting your CMS/SSG/templating engine/etc. because you’re not using one. It is utterly freeing.

Your landing page should be called index.html and should sit in the root level of your web root (which is /home/public if you’re using NearlyFreeSpeech).

Blog Posts

Blog posts are just web pages. These are super easy to maintain and publish. You can run with any process you’d like, but the simplest approach is to just make a copy of the web page for your last blog post, give it a unique file name, and then edit it with your new blog post content.

This is a good time to think about your site structure and how you want to organize your files. The URLs to your posts will reflect the file structure on the disk. Do you want all of your blog posts to be in a /blog/ section? Then make a blog folder in your web root and store your blog files there. You can name your posts with the desired slug, e.g. the-best-lunch-i-ever-had.html, or you could make folders for them and store the blog posts inside each folder as an index.html file to hide the .html file extension (but contrary to popular belief, it’s a beautiful and honest file extension).

This is also a good time to consider another benefit of Hardcore IndieWeb. When your posts are individual HTML files (not Markdown files or database entries), they can finally be seen as the individual web pages that they truly are. And that means that you can really lean into making all of your posts unique! They don’t all have to be cookie-cutter paper-doll clones of one another; that’s just a bi-product of modern web publishing tools and the cultural influence that they have on our concept of a “blog”. You can now go ahead and make every blog post as special and individual as you’d like. Each post can have its own personality, baked right into its HTML. Individual style, individual appearance, even individual layout. Literally everything is possible with this approach.

Archive Page

Managing your archive page is simple. Make a folder called archive (or whatever you’d like to call it), add an index.html page there, and list your blog posts on the page. You can organize them in any way you wish. You can highlight your favorite posts at the top of the page. You can do anything—it’s your archive page to manage as you please.

Feed

An RSS feed isn’t some magical esoteric web code thing; it’s just a file on disk. And it’s a file that is in a fairly simple format to parse and work with, and you can totally do that in your text editor. It’s really not difficult.

You can get started with a simple Atom feed (100% RSS-compatible and universally supported) by visiting the Atom feed page on Wikipedia, copying the example feed shown halfway down the page, and pasting that into a feed.xml file (or whatever you want to call it). Then just change all of the example.com references and <title>, <subtitle>, etc. values to reflect your own domain and information. Finally, just include an <entry> section for each post that you want to include in your feed. Set the relevant data within each (dates/times, title, summary, etc.). For the <id>, just grab a fresh UUID. And when you’re finished, copy and paste your feed into the W3C Feed Validation Service to make sure that it can be parsed. If there are any issues, the validator will tell you what needs to be corrected.

Uploading

This is the easiest part! When you’re ready to publish your website, just use your file transfer program to connect to the server and drag your files into the remote server. The first time you publish to your web host, you’ll want to copy everything over. But on subsequent uploads, you can just transfer anything that’s new or changed (e.g. your landing page, new blog post, feed, and archive page).

Next Steps

At this point, you have a fully functioning website that lives primarily on your own computer and is completely portable. This is the Hardcore IndieWeb way. You’re ready for anything: if your host vanished, you can just use another host and upload your website there. You don’t have to worry about critical security vulnerabilities in blogging software or SSG dependencies or anything like that. You are in complete control over every aspect of your web content, and you’re reliant upon no one else.

Where do you go from here? That’s up to you. You don’t have to go anywhere at all. You can follow this exact process forever and you’ll always have a wonderful website that is entirely your own and completely independent.

You could also choose to explore additional tools and processes to support your own workflow. Keep in mind that everything you add creates a dependency on something else, but as long as your content continues to live primarily on your own device and you have a full copy of your ready-to-publish website, you’re A-OK as far as Hardcore IndieWeb goes.

Conclusion

“Did I just read 2,500 words that tell me to write my own HTML and upload it to a web server?” Yeah. You did.

If this seems like ridiculous advice in 2026, I think that’s largely the result of how we’ve strayed so far from the original simplicity we had when working with the web. The layers of technical complexity introduced over the past 30 years, the new additive processes, and the expectations that come with it all combine into a modern approach that turns over more of our control and independence to other people than at any other point in the web’s history. Even under the “IndieWeb” umbrella, so many people have their only copy of their entire web presence entrusted to some guy on the internet who’s promised to take care of it. That’s not real independence, and we should stop pretending that it is.

Hardcore IndieWeb may not be for everyone. But if you care about your words, who holds them, and where and how they appear online, then you might just find that it’s for you. And if while embracing this particular approach you happen to reconnect with the web in a way that so many of us enjoyed early on, experiencing the magic and delight of working directly with HTML and copying files to your own space on a web host, then that’s a heck of an added bonus. Hardcore IndieWeb may sound intense, but the reality is that it’s empowering, fun, and rewarding. And you deserve that.

sentences.zh: IndieWeb 精神旨在消除对您和您的东西的外部影响和控制。

blocks.html_en: And as with any movement, there are a bunch of different services out there aiming to help you plug into this modern independent web. I even run one myself! But I always place my values over profit, so I don’t have any problem sharing the simple truth that you don’t need a fancy subscription blogging service to join the IndieWeb. In fact, when you use one of those services, you’re actually taking a step back from real independence, because your content often winds up living in someone else’s database on someone else’s server. They’ll tell you that’s OK because you can always export it whenever you’d like, in open formats, and of course that’s nice. But you still don’t fully fully own and control your content when you’re dependent on someone else’s service.

blocks.html_en: Defining Hardcore IndieWeb

sentences.en: Defining Hardcore IndieWeb
sentences.zh: 定义硬核独立网络

blocks.html_en: Hardcore IndieWeb fully embraces existing IndieWeb principles; there’s really nothing new or different there. The key aspects of Hardcore IndieWeb are control over your content and portability of your website:

sentences.en: Hardcore IndieWeb fully embraces existing IndieWeb principles; there’s really nothing new or different there.
sentences.zh: 硬核 IndieWeb 完全拥抱现有的 IndieWeb 原则；那里确实没有什么新的或不同的。

sentences.en: The key aspects of Hardcore IndieWeb are control over your content and portability of your website:
sentences.zh: Hardcore IndieWeb 的关键方面是对网站内容的控制和可移植性：

blocks.html_en: • If your content doesn’t primarily exist on your own hard drive, you don’t fully control your content.

sentences.en: If your content doesn’t primarily exist on your own hard drive, you don’t fully control your content.
sentences.zh: 如果您的内容主要不存在于您自己的硬盘上，则您无法完全控制您的内容。

blocks.html_en: • If you don’t have a copy of your published HTML and web assets on your own hard drive, it’s not truly portable.

sentences.en: If you don’t have a copy of your published HTML and web assets on your own hard drive, it’s not truly portable.
sentences.zh: 如果您自己的硬盘上没有已发布的 HTML 和 Web 资源的副本，那么它就不是真正的可移植的。

blocks.html_en: Why does this matter? Consider what happens if the IndieWeb blogging service you’re using goes belly-up one day. Your ability to move to a new service depends entirely on your ability to export your data. What if you can’t export it? The service provider may have told you that you own your content, but what good does that do if you can’t access it?

sentences.en: Why does this matter?
sentences.zh: 为什么这很重要？

sentences.en: Consider what happens if the IndieWeb blogging service you’re using goes belly-up one day.
sentences.zh: 想象一下，如果您正在使用的 IndieWeb 博客服务有一天崩溃了，会发生什么。

sentences.en: Your ability to move to a new service depends entirely on your ability to export your data.
sentences.zh: 您迁移到新服务的能力完全取决于您导出数据的能力。

sentences.en: What if you can’t export it?
sentences.zh: 如果无法导出怎么办？

sentences.en: The service provider may have told you that you own your content, but what good does that do if you can’t access it?
sentences.zh: 服务提供商可能已经告诉您，您拥有自己的内容，但如果您无法访问它，那又有什么用呢？

blocks.html_en: From a portability standpoint, consider what happens if you discover that the owner of your IndieWeb blogging service has been outed as engaging in abhorrent behavior or just comes down with a major case of the ick (this has happened before!). Sure, you can export your data in an open format and move house. But now you have to find another service that can work with that format, or convert everything and adopt an entirely new set of tools or processes. And you have to move house, which is a major pain.

sentences.en: From a portability standpoint, consider what happens if you discover that the owner of your IndieWeb blogging service has been outed as engaging in abhorrent behavior or just comes down with a major case of the ick (this has happened before!).

sentences.zh: 当然，您可以以开放格式导出数据并搬家。

sentences.en: And you have to move house, which is a major pain.
sentences.zh: 而且你必须搬家，这是一个很大的痛苦。

blocks.html_en: If you follow Hardcore IndieWeb, neither of these situations can affect you. Your content remains completely under your own control at all times, and your website exists in a fully published format at all times. The independence is tangible, and you can benefit from it whenever necessary.

sentences.en: If you follow Hardcore IndieWeb, neither of these situations can affect you.
sentences.zh: 如果您关注 Hardcore IndieWeb，那么这两种情况都不会影响您。

sentences.en: Your content remains completely under your own control at all times, and your website exists in a fully published format at all times.
sentences.zh: 您的内容始终完全由您自己控制，并且您的网站始终以完全发布的格式存在。

sentences.en: The independence is tangible, and you can benefit from it whenever necessary.
sentences.zh: 独立性是有形的，您可以在必要时从中受益。

blocks.html_en: The methodology

sentences.en: The methodology
sentences.zh: 方法论

blocks.html_en: In many ways—no, actually, in every way—Hardcore IndieWeb mirrors the very same web publishing practices that were used in the 90s when the web was brand new. These were things that I did back then myself, every day, and they worked great! And they still work today, of course, but few people would recognize the approach because we’re now multiple levels deep in hybrid SaaS CMS/SSG solutions tethered to half a dozen markup languages and twice as many template systems. We’re living in a messy and complex web right now, but it doesn’t have to be that way. The complexity is one choice, and the simplicity of Hardcore IndieWeb is a different choice.

blocks.html_en: First, you’ll need to find a text editor that you like. Hopefully you already have one, but if not, you can try different editors until you find one that works for you. (I use Nova by Panic, myself.) You can use virtually any editor, because it only needs to support saving files to your disk (and they all do that).

FileZilla is a nice cross-platform app. You can use anything that can transfer files over SSH (or the SFTP protocol).

If you’ve always assumed that you need a special blogging service or software to maintain these items, that’s totally understandable. But you don’t! blocks.html_en: A Quick Note About Web Pages

sentences.en: A Quick Note About Web Pages
sentences.zh: 关于网页的快速说明

blocks.html_en: If you already have your existing web site or blog in HTML format, you’re in a great position to get started with Hardcore IndieWeb. If you don’t, that’s OK. Depending on where your blog lives today, there are likely ways to export it in HTML or to convert it into HTML. If your blog is huge, you’ll probably want to use a tool to make this easier. If it’s small, maybe you’d enjoy diving into creating your new HTML files yourself (which is always a fun way to revisit your posts and such).

sentences.zh: 如果您的博客很大，您可能需要使用一个工具来简化此操作。

sentences.en: If it’s small, maybe you’d enjoy diving into creating your new HTML files yourself (which is always a fun way to revisit your posts and such).
sentences.zh: 如果它很小，也许您会喜欢亲自创建新的 HTML 文件（这始终是重新访问您的帖子等的有趣方式）。

blocks.html_en: If you don’t know much HTML, you can totally learn it. Heck, I’ll even teach you if you’d like. It’s super fun to learn, and using HTML brings you so much closer to your web content in ways that modern tools just don’t capture. Markdown may be many people’s preferred way to author content, but HTML is the language of the web, and I can personally attest to the fact that there are plenty of occasions where doing something in plain HTML turned out to be far easier than fighting a Markdown parser.

sentences.en: If you don’t know much HTML, you can totally learn it.
sentences.zh: 如果你不太了解 HTML，你完全可以学习它。

sentences.zh: 哎呀，如果你愿意的话，我什至可以教你。

sentences.en: It’s super fun to learn, and using HTML brings you so much closer to your web content in ways that modern tools just don’t capture.
sentences.zh: 学习起来非常有趣，并且使用 HTML 可以让您以现代工具无法捕获的方式更接近您的 Web 内容。

blocks.html_en: Anyway. If you want to jump in with some ready-made web material to use as a basis for your site, there are a bajillion free web designs and templates out there. Here’s one I just found. You can download something you like, open it in your text editor, and make it your very own.

blocks.html_en: Landing Page

sentences.en: Landing Page
sentences.zh: 登陆页面

blocks.html_en: Your landing page is your home on the web, and you can do whatever you’d like with it. It can show your latest blog post, or several of them, or none of them at all. It can show entire posts or just the first couple of sentences. You’re in complete control here, and you can put whatever you want on that page. If you want to display your latest blog post, just copy and paste it there and add a link to its standalone page. If you want to show your five most recent posts, paste the new one at the top and delete the oldest one at the bottom. You’re now working directly with your content on your own landing page, and you can do whatever you’d like, however you’d like.

sentences.en: You’re now working directly with your content on your own landing page, and you can do whatever you’d like, however you’d like.
sentences.zh: 现在，您可以直接在自己的着陆页上处理内容，并且可以随心所欲地做任何您想做的事情。

blocks.html_en: This might sound like madness, if you’re fully accustomed to today’s modern template-driven systems for managing blogs and web content. And that’s a totally fair way to feel about it. But consider that this approach offers some unique possibilities that are actually harder to do with template-driven solutions. With this approach, you’re in a position to deviate from the norm. You can take things in different directions. You can apply your creativity in fun and interesting ways, and you’ll never find yourself fighting your CMS/SSG/templating engine/etc. because you’re not using one. It is utterly freeing.

blocks.html_en: Your landing page should be called index.html and should sit in the root level of your web root (which is /home/public if you’re using NearlyFreeSpeech).

blocks.html_en: Blog Posts

sentences.en: Blog Posts
sentences.zh: 博客文章

blocks.html_en: Blog posts are just web pages. These are super easy to maintain and publish. You can run with any process you’d like, but the simplest approach is to just make a copy of the web page for your last blog post, give it a unique file name, and then edit it with your new blog post content.

sentences.en: Blog posts are just web pages.
sentences.zh: 博客文章只是网页。

sentences.en: These are super easy to maintain and publish.
sentences.zh: 这些非常容易维护和发布。

sentences.en: You can run with any process you’d like, but the simplest approach is to just make a copy of the web page for your last blog post, give it a unique file name, and then edit it with your new blog post content.
sentences.zh: 您可以运行任何您想要的进程，但最简单的方法是复制上一篇博客文章的网页，为其指定一个唯一的文件名，然后使用新的博客文章内容对其进行编辑。

blocks.html_en: This is a good time to think about your site structure and how you want to organize your files. The URLs to your posts will reflect the file structure on the disk. Do you want all of your blog posts to be in a /blog/ section? Then make a blog folder in your web root and store your blog files there. You can name your posts with the desired slug, e.g. the-best-lunch-i-ever-had.html, or you could make folders for them and store the blog posts inside each folder as an index.html file to hide the .html file extension (but contrary to popular belief, it’s a beautiful and honest file extension).

sentences.en: This is a good time to think about your site structure and how you want to organize your files.
sentences.zh: 现在是考虑站点结构以及如何组织文件的好时机。

sentences.en: The URLs to your posts will reflect the file structure on the disk.
sentences.zh: 您的帖子的 URL 将反映磁盘上的文件结构。

sentences.en: Do you want all of your blog posts to be in a /blog/ section?
sentences.zh: 您希望所有博客文章都位于 /blog/ 部分吗？

sentences.en: Then make a blog folder in your web root and store your blog files there.
sentences.zh: 然后在您的网络根目录中创建一个博客文件夹并在其中存储您的博客文件。

sentences.en: You can name your posts with the desired slug, e.g. the-best-lunch-i-ever-had.html, or you could make folders for them and store the blog posts inside each folder as an index.html file to hide the .html file extension (but contrary to popular belief, it’s a beautiful and honest file extension).
sentences.zh: 您可以使用所需的名称来命名您的帖子，例如the-best-lunch-i-ever-had.html，或者您可以为它们创建文件夹，并将博客文章作为 index.html 文件存储在每个文件夹中，以隐藏 .html 文件扩展名（但与普遍看法相反，这是一个美丽而诚实的文件扩展名）。

blocks.html_en: This is also a good time to consider another benefit of Hardcore IndieWeb. When your posts are individual HTML files (not Markdown files or database entries), they can finally be seen as the individual web pages that they truly are. And that means that you can really lean into making all of your posts unique! They don’t all have to be cookie-cutter paper-doll clones of one another; that’s just a bi-product of modern web publishing tools and the cultural influence that they have on our concept of a “blog”. You can now go ahead and make every blog post as special and individual as you’d like. Each post can have its own personality, baked right into its HTML. Individual style, individual appearance, even individual layout. Literally everything is possible with this approach.

sentences.en: This is also a good time to consider another benefit of Hardcore IndieWeb.
sentences.zh: 这也是考虑 Hardcore IndieWeb 的另一个好处的好时机。

sentences.en: When your posts are individual HTML files (not Markdown files or database entries), they can finally be seen as the individual web pages that they truly are.
sentences.zh: 当您的帖子是单独的 HTML 文件（不是 Markdown 文件或数据库条目）时，它们最终可以被视为真正的单独网页。

sentences.en: And that means that you can really lean into making all of your posts unique!
sentences.zh: 这意味着您可以真正致力于让您的所有帖子变得独一无二！

sentences.en: They don’t all have to be cookie-cutter paper-doll clones of one another; that’s just a bi-product of modern web publishing tools and the cultural influence that they have on our concept of a “blog”.
sentences.zh: 它们不必都是千篇一律的纸娃娃克隆体；这只是现代网络发布工具及其对我们“博客”概念的文化影响的副产品。

sentences.en: You can now go ahead and make every blog post as special and individual as you’d like.
sentences.zh: 现在，您可以继续按照您的意愿将每一篇博客文章变得特别且个性化。

sentences.en: Each post can have its own personality, baked right into its HTML.
sentences.zh: 每个帖子都可以有自己的个性，直接融入到 HTML 中。

sentences.en: Individual style, individual appearance, even individual layout.
sentences.zh: 个性化的风格，个性化的外观，甚至个性化的布局。

sentences.en: Literally everything is possible with this approach.
sentences.zh: 从字面上看，通过这种方法一切皆有可能。

blocks.html_en: Archive Page

sentences.en: Archive Page
sentences.zh: 存档页面

blocks.html_en: Managing your archive page is simple. Make a folder called archive (or whatever you’d like to call it), add an index.html page there, and list your blog posts on the page. You can organize them in any way you wish. You can highlight your favorite posts at the top of the page. You can do anything—it’s your archive page to manage as you please.

sentences.en: You can organize them in any way you wish.
sentences.zh: 您可以按照您希望的任何方式组织它们。

sentences.en: You can highlight your favorite posts at the top of the page.
sentences.zh: 您可以在页面顶部突出显示您最喜欢的帖子。

sentences.en: You can do anything—it’s your archive page to manage as you please.
sentences.zh: 您可以做任何事情——这是您可以随意管理的存档页面。

blocks.html_en: Feed

An RSS feed isn’t some magical esoteric web code thing; it’s just a file on disk. And it’s a file that is in a fairly simple format to parse and work with, and you can totally do that in your text editor. It’s really not difficult.

sentences.en: An RSS feed isn’t some magical esoteric web code thing; it’s just a file on disk.
sentences.zh: RSS 提要并不是什么神奇深奥的网络代码；它是一种内容。它只是磁盘上的一个文件。

sentences.en: And it’s a file that is in a fairly simple format to parse and work with, and you can totally do that in your text editor.
sentences.zh: 它的文件格式相当简单，易于解析和使用，您完全可以在文本编辑器中完成此操作。

sentences.en: It’s really not difficult.
sentences.zh: 这确实不难。

blocks.html_en: You can get started with a simple Atom feed (100% RSS-compatible and universally supported) by visiting the Atom feed page on Wikipedia, copying the example feed shown halfway down the page, and pasting that into a feed.xml file (or whatever you want to call it). Then just change all of the example.com references and <title>, <subtitle>, etc. values to reflect your own domain and information. Finally, just include an <entry> section for each post that you want to include in your feed. Set the relevant data within each (dates/times, title, summary, etc.). For the <id>, just grab a fresh UUID. And when you’re finished, copy and paste your feed into the W3C Feed Validation Service to make sure that it can be parsed. If there are any issues, the validator will tell you what needs to be corrected.

sentences.en: You can get started with a simple Atom feed (100% RSS-compatible and universally supported) by visiting the Atom feed page on Wikipedia, copying the example feed shown halfway down the page, and pasting that into a feed.xml file (or whatever you want to call it).
sentences.zh: 您可以通过访问 Wikipedia 上的 Atom feed 页面，复制页面中间显示的示例 feed，并将其粘贴到 feed.xml 文件（或任何您想要的名称）中，开始使用简单的 Atom feed（100% RSS 兼容且普遍支持）。

sentences.zh: 最后，只需为您想要包含在 Feed 中的每篇帖子添加一个 <entry> 部分即可。

sentences.en: Set the relevant data within each (dates/times, title, summary, etc.).
sentences.zh: 设置每个数据中的相关数据（日期/时间、标题、摘要等）。

sentences.en: For the <id>, just grab a fresh UUID.
sentences.zh: 对于 <id>，只需获取一个新的 UUID。

sentences.en: And when you’re finished, copy and paste your feed into the W3C Feed Validation Service to make sure that it can be parsed.
sentences.zh: 完成后，将您的 Feed 复制并粘贴到 W3C Feed 验证服务中，以确保它可以被解析。

sentences.en: If there are any issues, the validator will tell you what needs to be corrected.
sentences.zh: 如果有任何问题，验证器会告诉您需要纠正哪些问题。

blocks.html_en: Uploading

sentences.en: Uploading
sentences.zh: 上传中

blocks.html_en: This is the easiest part! When you’re ready to publish your website, just use your file transfer program to connect to the server and drag your files into the remote server. The first time you publish to your web host, you’ll want to copy everything over. But on subsequent uploads, you can just transfer anything that’s new or changed (e.g. your landing page, new blog post, feed, and archive page).

sentences.en: This is the easiest part!
sentences.zh: 这是最简单的部分！

sentences.en: When you’re ready to publish your website, just use your file transfer program to connect to the server and drag your files into the remote server.
sentences.zh: 当您准备好发布网站时，只需使用文件传输程序连接到服务器并将文件拖到远程服务器中。

sentences.en: The first time you publish to your web host, you’ll want to copy everything over.
sentences.zh: 第一次发布到网络托管服务商时，您需要复制所有内容。

sentences.en: But on subsequent uploads, you can just transfer anything that’s new or changed (e.g. your landing page, new blog post, feed, and archive page).
sentences.zh: 但在后续上传中，您可以只传输任何新的或更改的内容（例如您的登陆页面、新博客文章、提要和存档页面）。

blocks.html_en: Next Steps

sentences.en: Next Steps
sentences.zh: 下一步

blocks.html_en: At this point, you have a fully functioning website that lives primarily on your own computer and is completely portable. This is the Hardcore IndieWeb way. You’re ready for anything: if your host vanished, you can just use another host and upload your website there. You don’t have to worry about critical security vulnerabilities in blogging software or SSG dependencies or anything like that. You are in complete control over every aspect of your web content, and you’re reliant upon no one else.

sentences.en: At this point, you have a fully functioning website that lives primarily on your own computer and is completely portable.
sentences.zh: 此时，您已经拥有一个功能齐全的网站，该网站主要位于您自己的计算机上并且完全可移植。

sentences.en: This is the Hardcore IndieWeb way.
sentences.zh: 这就是硬核 IndieWeb 方式。

sentences.en: You’re ready for anything: if your host vanished, you can just use another host and upload your website there.
sentences.zh: 您已经做好了一切准备：如果您的主机消失了，您可以使用另一个主机并在那里上传您的网站。

sentences.en: You don’t have to worry about critical security vulnerabilities in blogging software or SSG dependencies or anything like that.
sentences.zh: 您不必担心博客软件或 SSG 依赖项或类似内容中的严重安全漏洞。

sentences.en: You are in complete control over every aspect of your web content, and you’re reliant upon no one else.
sentences.zh: 您可以完全控制网络内容的各个方面，并且不依赖于任何其他人。

blocks.html_en: Where do you go from here? That’s up to you. You don’t have to go anywhere at all. You can follow this exact process forever and you’ll always have a wonderful website that is entirely your own and completely independent.

sentences.en: Where do you go from here?
sentences.zh: 从这里你要去哪里？

sentences.en: That’s up to you.
sentences.zh: 这取决于你。

sentences.en: You don’t have to go anywhere at all.
sentences.zh: 您根本不必去任何地方。

sentences.en: You can follow this exact process forever and you’ll always have a wonderful website that is entirely your own and completely independent.
sentences.zh: 您可以永远遵循这个确切的过程，您将永远拥有一个完全属于您自己且完全独立的精彩网站。

blocks.html_en: You could also choose to explore additional tools and processes to support your own workflow. Keep in mind that everything you add creates a dependency on something else, but as long as your content continues to live primarily on your own device and you have a full copy of your ready-to-publish website, you’re A-OK as far as Hardcore IndieWeb goes.

sentences.en: You could also choose to explore additional tools and processes to support your own workflow.
sentences.zh: 您还可以选择探索其他工具和流程来支持您自己的工作流程。

sentences.en: Keep in mind that everything you add creates a dependency on something else, but as long as your content continues to live primarily on your own device and you have a full copy of your ready-to-publish website, you’re A-OK as far as Hardcore IndieWeb goes.
sentences.zh: 请记住，您添加的所有内容都会产生对其他内容的依赖，但只要您的内容继续主要存在于您自己的设备上，并且您拥有准备发布网站的完整副本，就 Hardcore IndieWeb 而言，您就没有问题。

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: “Did I just read 2,500 words that tell me to write my own HTML and upload it to a web server?” Yeah. You did.

sentences.en: “Did I just read 2,500 words that tell me to write my own HTML and upload it to a web server?”
sentences.zh: “我刚刚读了 2,500 个单词，告诉我编写自己的 HTML 并将其上传到网络服务器吗？”

sentences.en: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: You did.
sentences.zh: 你做到了。

blocks.html_en: If this seems like ridiculous advice in 2026, I think that’s largely the result of how we’ve strayed so far from the original simplicity we had when working with the web. The layers of technical complexity introduced over the past 30 years, the new additive processes, and the expectations that come with it all combine into a modern approach that turns over more of our control and independence to other people than at any other point in the web’s history. Even under the “IndieWeb” umbrella, so many people have their only copy of their entire web presence entrusted to some guy on the internet who’s promised to take care of it. That’s not real independence, and we should stop pretending that it is.

sentences.en: If this seems like ridiculous advice in 2026, I think that’s largely the result of how we’ve strayed so far from the original simplicity we had when working with the web.
sentences.zh: 如果这个建议在 2026 年看起来很荒谬，我认为这很大程度上是因为我们已经偏离了使用网络时最初的简单性。

sentences.en: The layers of technical complexity introduced over the past 30 years, the new additive processes, and the expectations that come with it all combine into a modern approach that turns over more of our control and independence to other people than at any other point in the web’s history.
sentences.zh: 过去 30 年引入的技术复杂性层次、新的附加流程以及随之而来的期望，所有这些都结合成一种现代方法，将我们的控制权和独立性移交给其他人，这比网络历史上的任何其他时刻都多。

sentences.en: Even under the “IndieWeb” umbrella, so many people have their only copy of their entire web presence entrusted to some guy on the internet who’s promised to take care of it.
sentences.zh: 即使在“IndieWeb”的保护伞下，很多人也将他们整个网络存在的唯一副本委托给互联网上承诺照顾它的某个人。

sentences.en: That’s not real independence, and we should stop pretending that it is.
sentences.zh: 这不是真正的独立，我们不应该再假装它是真正的独立。

blocks.html_en: Hardcore IndieWeb may not be for everyone. But if you care about your words, who holds them, and where and how they appear online, then you might just find that it’s for you. And if while embracing this particular approach you happen to reconnect with the web in a way that so many of us enjoyed early on, experiencing the magic and delight of working directly with HTML and copying files to your own space on a web host, then that’s a heck of an added bonus. Hardcore IndieWeb may sound intense, but the reality is that it’s empowering, fun, and rewarding. And you deserve that.

sentences.en: Hardcore IndieWeb may not be for everyone.
sentences.zh: 硬核 IndieWeb 可能不适合所有人。

sentences.en: But if you care about your words, who holds them, and where and how they appear online, then you might just find that it’s for you.
sentences.zh: 但如果您关心您的文字、谁拥有它们以及它们在网上出现的位置和方式，那么您可能会发现它适合您。

content_html_zh:
亚当·纽博尔德 2026 年 7 月 18 日

如果您不熟悉 IndieWeb 运动，那么这是一种保持您在网络上存在感的实用方法，强调完全拥有您的身份和内容。 现在，当越来越多的公司希望为您拥有和控制这些东西时，这一点尤其重要。 IndieWeb 精神旨在消除对您和您的东西的外部影响和控制。

与任何运动一样，有许多不同的服务旨在帮助您接入这个现代独立网络。 我什至自己也经营一个！ 但我总是将自己的价值观置于利润之上，因此我可以毫不犹豫地分享一个简单的事实：您不需要花哨的订阅博客服务即可加入 IndieWeb。 事实上，当您使用其中一项服务时，您实际上是从真正的独立性中退了一步，因为您的内容通常最终存在于其他人服务器上的其他人的数据库中。 他们会告诉你没关系，因为你可以随时以开放格式导出它，这当然很好。 但是，当您依赖他人的服务时，您仍然无法完全拥有和控制您的内容。

需要明确的是，使用这些服务没有任何问题。 如果您已经在使用它们并且对它们感到满意，那就太好了！ 本指南不适合您。 但是，如果完全独立并控制网络内容的想法对您有吸引力，请继续阅读。 因为这就是 Hardcore IndieWeb 的全部意义所在。

定义硬核独立网络

硬核 IndieWeb 完全拥抱现有的 IndieWeb 原则；那里确实没有什么新的或不同的。 Hardcore IndieWeb 的关键方面是对网站内容的控制和可移植性：

  • 如果您的内容主要不存在于您自己的硬盘上，则您无法完全控制您的内容。
  • 如果您自己的硬盘上没有已发布的 HTML 和 Web 资源的副本，那么它就不是真正的可移植的。

为什么这很重要？ 想象一下，如果您正在使用的 IndieWeb 博客服务有一天崩溃了，会发生什么。 您迁移到新服务的能力完全取决于您导出数据的能力。 如果无法导出怎么办？ 服务提供商可能已经告诉您，您拥有自己的内容，但如果您无法访问它，那又有什么用呢？

从可移植性的角度来看，考虑一下如果您发现您的 IndieWeb 博客服务的所有者因从事令人憎恶的行为而被揭发或只是遭遇重大案件（这种情况以前发生过！），会发生什么。 当然，您可以以开放格式导出数据并搬家。 但现在您必须找到另一种可以使用该格式的服务，或者转换所有内容并采用一套全新的工具或流程。 而且你必须搬家，这是一个很大的痛苦。

如果您关注 Hardcore IndieWeb，那么这两种情况都不会影响您。 您的内容始终完全由您自己控制，并且您的网站始终以完全发布的格式存在。 独立性是有形的，您可以在必要时从中受益。

方法论

在很多方面（不，实际上是在各个方面），硬核独立网络反映了 90 年代网络全新时所使用的网络出版实践。 这些都是我当时自己每天都会做的事情，而且效果很好！ 当然，它们今天仍然有效，但很少有人会认识到这种方法，因为我们现在处于混合 SaaS CMS/SSG 解决方案的多个层次，与六种标记语言和两倍的模板系统相关。 我们现在生活在一个混乱而复杂的网络中，但事实并非一定如此。 复杂性是一种选择，而 Hardcore IndieWeb 的简单性则是另一种选择。

该过程如下所示：

  1. 在您的硬盘上创作您的网络内容。
  2. 在网络浏览器中预览它。
  3. 当您对所拥有的内容感到满意时，请将其上传到您的网络托管服务商。 根据需要重复多次。

就是这样。 确实就是这么简单。 除了域名之外，您还需要：

  • 文本编辑器
  • 一个文件传输工具
  • 网络主机

再说一次，仅此而已。 您不需要任何其他特殊软件。 没有编程环境或 IDE，没有框架、shell 或 CLI 工具。 而且绝对没有每月订阅。 在下一节中，我将介绍如何选择这些项目，并且我会推荐一个非常好的网络主机，每天只需 0.01 美元，您可以即用即付（因此无需订阅）。

（您确实还需要了解一些 HTML，但每个人都可以学习 HTML。 事实上，您只需了解几个标签并进行大量复制和粘贴就可以逃脱惩罚，所以不要担心！）

入门

首先，您需要找到一个您喜欢的文本编辑器。 希望您已经拥有一个，但如果没有，您可以尝试不同的编辑器，直到找到适合您的编辑器。 （我自己使用 Panic 的 Nova。） 您几乎可以使用任何编辑器，因为它只需要支持将文件保存到磁盘（它们都这样做）。

然后，您需要一种传输文件的方法。 我使用 Nova 作为编辑器的部分原因是它也可以处理文件传输。 但有很多选择。 FileZilla 是一个不错的跨平台应用程序。 您可以使用任何可以通过 SSH（或 SFTP 协议）传输文件的东西。

最后，您需要一个地方来托管您的网络内容。 那里有无数的网络主机，它们都具有不同的功能和优点。 但对于Hardcore IndieWeb，你只需要基础知识，根本不需要花很多钱。 只需每天 0.01 美元，您就可以在 NearlyFreeSpeech.net 上运行一个静态网站，这就是我的推荐。 自 2008 年以来我就拥有了他们的活跃账户，他们非常棒！

不过，您可以使用任何您喜欢的网络主机。 如果您想使用 NearlyFreeSpeech，请继续注册，为您的帐户注资（只需 0.25 美元即可启动），添加一个网站（静态、非生产），然后就可以开始了。 单击站点名称后，您将在“站点”选项卡上找到站点的登录信息（用于文件传输）。 您将获得一个免费的子域来使用，但如果您愿意，您可以在“域”选项卡上添加您自己的个人域（并且您完全应该这样做）。

过程

一旦您拥有编辑器、传输文件的方式以及网络主机的活动帐户，您就可以开始了。 至此，世界尽在你的掌握之中。 您可以运行任何类型的网站，但对于本指南，我们假设您想运行博客。

大多数时候，博客由以下内容组成：

  • 登陆页面
  • 帖子/文章
  • 存档页面
  • A feed

如果您一直认为需要特殊的博客服务或软件来维护这些项目，那是完全可以理解的。 但你没有！ 您实际上可以自己管理这些，而且非常简单。 此时，您可能（可以理解）想知道“什么样的人会在文本编辑器中管理自己的 RSS 提要？” 答案就是你。 您是那种完全有能力在自己的编辑器中管理自己的 RSS 提要的人。

我们很快就会介绍这一点。 首先，我们来介绍一下 HTML 页面。

关于网页的快速说明

如果您已经拥有 HTML 格式的现有网站或博客，那么您就可以很好地开始使用 Hardcore IndieWeb。 如果你不这样做，也没关系。 根据您的博客目前所处的位置，可能有多种方法可以将其导出为 HTML 格式或将其转换为 HTML。 如果您的博客很大，您可能需要使用一个工具来简化此操作。 如果它很小，也许您会喜欢亲自创建新的 HTML 文件（这始终是重新访问您的帖子等的有趣方式）。

如果你不太了解 HTML，你完全可以学习它。 哎呀，如果你愿意的话，我什至可以教你。 学习起来非常有趣，并且使用 HTML 可以让您以现代工具无法捕获的方式更接近您的 Web 内容。 Markdown 可能是许多人创作内容的首选方式，但 HTML 是网络语言，我个人可以证明，在很多情况下，用纯 HTML 做一些事情比与 Markdown 解析器进行斗争要容易得多。

反正。 如果您想使用一些现成的网页材料作为您网站的基础，那么这里有大量的免费网页设计和模板。 这是我刚刚找到的一个。 您可以下载您喜欢的内容，在文本编辑器中打开它，然后将其变成您自己的。

登陆页面

您的登陆页面是您在网络上的家，您可以用它做任何您想做的事情。 它可以显示您最新的博客文章，或其中几篇，或根本不显示。 它可以显示整个帖子或仅显示前几句话。 您在这里拥有完全的控制权，您可以在该页面上放置任何您想要的内容。 如果您想显示最新的博客文章，只需将其复制并粘贴到此处，然后添加指向其独立页面的链接即可。 如果您想显示最近的五篇帖子，请将新帖子粘贴到顶部并删除底部最旧的帖子。 现在，您可以直接在自己的着陆页上处理内容，并且可以随心所欲地做任何您想做的事情。

如果您完全习惯了当今用于管理博客和网络内容的现代模板驱动系统，这可能听起来很疯狂。 这是一种完全公平的感受方式。 但请考虑到这种方法提供了一些独特的可能性，而使用模板驱动的解决方案实际上很难做到这一点。 通过这种方法，你就可以偏离常态。 你可以把事情往不同的方向发展。 您可以以有趣的方式运用您的创造力，并且您永远不会发现自己与 CMS/SSG/模板引擎等作斗争。因为你没有使用它。 这是完全自由的。

您的登陆页面应称为index.html，并且应位于网络根目录的根级别（如果您使用的是NearlyFreeSpeech，则为/home/public）。

博客文章

博客文章只是网页。 这些非常容易维护和发布。 您可以运行任何您想要的进程，但最简单的方法是复制上一篇博客文章的网页，为其指定一个唯一的文件名，然后使用新的博客文章内容对其进行编辑。

现在是考虑站点结构以及如何组织文件的好时机。 您的帖子的 URL 将反映磁盘上的文件结构。 您希望所有博客文章都位于 /blog/ 部分吗？ 然后在您的网络根目录中创建一个博客文件夹并在其中存储您的博客文件。 您可以使用所需的名称来命名您的帖子，例如the-best-lunch-i-ever-had.html，或者您可以为它们创建文件夹，并将博客文章作为 index.html 文件存储在每个文件夹中，以隐藏 .html 文件扩展名（但与普遍看法相反，这是一个美丽而诚实的文件扩展名）。

这也是考虑 Hardcore IndieWeb 的另一个好处的好时机。 当您的帖子是单独的 HTML 文件（不是 Markdown 文件或数据库条目）时，它们最终可以被视为真正的单独网页。 这意味着您可以真正致力于让您的所有帖子变得独一无二！ 它们不必都是千篇一律的纸娃娃克隆体；这只是现代网络发布工具及其对我们“博客”概念的文化影响的副产品。 现在，您可以继续按照您的意愿将每一篇博客文章变得特别且个性化。 每个帖子都可以有自己的个性，直接融入到 HTML 中。 个性化的风格，个性化的外观，甚至个性化的布局。 从字面上看，通过这种方法一切皆有可能。

存档页面

管理您的存档页面很简单。 创建一个名为 archive 的文件夹（或任何您喜欢的名称），在其中添加一个 index.html 页面，并在该页面上列出您的博客文章。 您可以按照您希望的任何方式组织它们。 您可以在页面顶部突出显示您最喜欢的帖子。 您可以做任何事情——这是您可以随意管理的存档页面。

Feed

RSS 提要并不是什么神奇深奥的网络代码；它是一种内容。它只是磁盘上的一个文件。 它的文件格式相当简单，易于解析和使用，您完全可以在文本编辑器中完成此操作。 这确实不难。

您可以通过访问 Wikipedia 上的 Atom feed 页面，复制页面中间显示的示例 feed，并将其粘贴到 feed.xml 文件（或任何您想要的名称）中，开始使用简单的 Atom feed（100% RSS 兼容且普遍支持）。 然后只需更改所有 example.com 引用和 <title>、<subtitle> 等值即可反映您自己的域和信息。 最后，只需为您想要包含在 Feed 中的每篇帖子添加一个 <entry> 部分即可。 设置每个数据中的相关数据（日期/时间、标题、摘要等）。 对于 <id>，只需获取一个新的 UUID。 完成后，将您的 Feed 复制并粘贴到 W3C Feed 验证服务中，以确保它可以被解析。 如果有任何问题，验证器会告诉您需要纠正哪些问题。

上传中

这是最简单的部分！ 当您准备好发布网站时，只需使用文件传输程序连接到服务器并将文件拖到远程服务器中。 第一次发布到网络托管服务商时，您需要复制所有内容。 但在后续上传中，您可以只传输任何新的或更改的内容（例如您的登陆页面、新博客文章、提要和存档页面）。

下一步

此时，您已经拥有一个功能齐全的网站，该网站主要位于您自己的计算机上并且完全可移植。 这就是硬核 IndieWeb 方式。 您已经做好了一切准备：如果您的主机消失了，您可以使用另一个主机并在那里上传您的网站。 您不必担心博客软件或 SSG 依赖项或类似内容中的严重安全漏洞。 您可以完全控制网络内容的各个方面，并且不依赖于任何其他人。

从这里你要去哪里？ 这取决于你。 您根本不必去任何地方。 您可以永远遵循这个确切的过程，您将永远拥有一个完全属于您自己且完全独立的精彩网站。

您还可以选择探索其他工具和流程来支持您自己的工作流程。 请记住，您添加的所有内容都会产生对其他内容的依赖，但只要您的内容继续主要存在于您自己的设备上，并且您拥有准备发布网站的完整副本，就 Hardcore IndieWeb 而言，您就没有问题。

结论

“我刚刚读了 2,500 个单词，告诉我编写自己的 HTML 并将其上传到网络服务器吗？” 是的。 你做到了。

如果这个建议在 2026 年看起来很荒谬，我认为这很大程度上是因为我们已经偏离了使用网络时最初的简单性。 过去 30 年引入的技术复杂性层次、新的附加流程以及随之而来的期望，所有这些都结合成一种现代方法，将我们的控制权和独立性移交给其他人，这比网络历史上的任何其他时刻都多。 即使在“IndieWeb”的保护伞下，很多人也将他们整个网络存在的唯一副本委托给互联网上承诺照顾它的某个人。 这不是真正的独立，我们不应该再假装它是真正的独立。

硬核 IndieWeb 可能不适合所有人。 但如果您关心您的文字、谁拥有它们以及它们在网上出现的位置和方式，那么您可能会发现它适合您。 如果在采用这种特殊方法的同时，您碰巧以我们很多人早期喜欢的方式重新连接到网络，体验直接使用 HTML 并将文件复制到网络主机上您自己的空间的魔力和乐趣，那么这就是一个额外的好处。 硬核独立网络听起来可能很激烈，但现实是它充满力量、有趣且有益。 这是你应得的。
